back: A simple graph G = (V, E) consists of a finite non-empty set V of vertices and a set E of unordered pairs of distinct vertices called edges.
A graph is connected if there exists a path between every pair of vertices. Otherwise, it is disconnected.
A graph is disconnected if there exists at least one pair of vertices with no path between them. It can be partitioned into two or more vertex sets with no edges between them.

front: Define a component of a graph
back: A component (or connected component) is a maximal connected subgraph of a graph. It is a connected subgraph that is not a proper subgraph of any other connected subgraph.
An isolated vertex is a vertex with degree zero (no edges incident to it). It forms a component by itself in a disconnected graph.
A pendant vertex (or leaf) is a vertex of degree 1, connected by exactly one edge to the rest of the graph.

front: Define a path in a graph
back: A path is a sequence of vertices where each adjacent pair is connected by an edge, with no vertex repeated (except possibly start = end for a cycle).
A cycle is a closed path that starts and ends at the same vertex, with all other vertices distinct and appearing only once.
A walk is a sequence of vertices and edges where vertices may repeat. A trail is a walk with no edge repeated.

front: What is the length of a path?
back: The length of a path is the number of edges in it, or equivalently, the number of vertices minus one.
Vertex connectivity is the minimum number of vertices whose removal disconnects the graph or reduces it to a single vertex.
Edge connectivity is the minimum number of edges whose removal disconnects the graph.

front: What is a complete graph Kₙ?
back: A complete graph Kₙ is a simple graph with n vertices where every pair of distinct vertices is connected by a unique edge. It is (n-1)-regular.
A bipartite graph is a graph whose vertex set can be partitioned into two disjoint sets such that every edge connects a vertex from one set to a vertex from the other set.
The sum of degrees of all vertices in a graph equals twice the number of edges: Σdeg(v) = 2|E|.
